video:video.video:title: Quarantine comes to an end for the last of the hantavirus ship passengers in Nebraska
video:video.video:description: The last eight American passengers who endured 42 days in a specialized hospital quarantine unit after exposure to an unusual hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship that killed three people have left the Nebraska facility.

video:video.video:title: Octavia Spencer (and the USA) are embracing World Cup fans
video:video.video:description: Octavia Spencer doesn't know the rules of soccer, but she's quickly learning as World Cup fever grips the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Against all odds, blind Kenyan tailor defies glaucoma to keep his business running
video:video.video:description: A tailor who lost his sight completely due to glaucoma has found ingenious ways to continue sewing accurately.

video:video.video:title: People living in attic apartments under zinc roofs of Paris suffer in the heat
video:video.video:description: Millions of people across Europe were exposed to extreme and exceptional high temperatures on Tuesday, with 40 fatalities from drowning recorded in France in the past week as residents seek relief from the searing heat.

video:video.video:title: A ransom note about Nancy Guthrie's disappearance says she died, CNN reports
video:video.video:description: A ransom note about Nancy Guthrie's disappearance says she died, CNN reports

video:video.video:title: The world’s oldest known football lands in Florida, and it tells a Scotland-Brazil story
video:video.video:description: A new exhibition at the Coral Gables Museum explores how soccer has connected cultures and countries for centuries.

video:video.video:title: Milwaukee Bucks fan reacts to Giannis Antetokounmpo trade
video:video.video:description: Milwaukee Bucks fan Danny Nelson reacts to Giannis Antetokounmpo being traded to the Miami Heat.

video:video.video:title: 8 convicted in Texas immigration center shooting and protest are sentenced to decades in prison
video:video.video:description: Eight protesters accused by the Justice Department of having ties to antifa have been sentenced to decades in federal prison over a shooting outside a Texas immigration detention center.

video:video.video:title: Hannah Waddingham's message for England in World Cup
video:video.video:description: British actor Hannah Waddingham, of "Ted Lasso" and "Ride or Die," offers her thoughts on the soccer World Cup.

video:video.video:title: 'Nobody cares': Trump interrupts Chris Wright as he attempts to get dates right
video:video.video:description: During a signing ceremony, the Energy Secretary offered an anecdote attempting to tie Einstein’s work to that of Trump’s uncle, who was an MIT professor.

video:video.video:title: Milly Alcock on fear of big 'Supergirl' role
video:video.video:description: "Supergirl" star Milly Alcock reveals why she was scared to join James Gunn's DC Universe.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 23
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Tuesday, June 23rd: Vance says Iran talks ‘created a good foundation’ for a deal; Pentagon says more funds needed to cover Iran war; Shooting inside Northern California library; Firefighters try to enter burning Los Angeles warehouse.

video:video.video:title: Spies and lies with "The Agency" stars
video:video.video:description: Trust no one? Well maybe not those who lie for a living, say Katherine Waterston and Michael Fassbender, stars of spy drama "The Agency."

video:video.video:title: Man arrested on suspicion of collecting bones from graves in Hungary
video:video.video:description: Police in Hungary have arrested a 30-year-old man who investigators say collected human body parts that he'd gathered from abandoned cemeteries and from his workplace at a hospital.

video:video.video:title: Christ the Redeemer lit in green and yellow as Rio celebrates Women’s World Cup countdown
video:video.video:description: Rio de Janeiro’s iconic Christ the Redeemer statue was lit up in green and yellow on Tuesday as the city kicked off its one-year countdown to the 2027 FIFA Women’s World Cup.

video:video.video:title: Trains halted across Germany because of communication system problem
video:video.video:description: A problem with a communications system forced Germany’s railway system to halt all trains, leaving passengers stranded across the country.

video:video.video:title: Fans react after England and Ghana play to 0-0 draw at World Cup despite flurry-filled final minutes
video:video.video:description: Ghana soccer fans celebrated after their team drew 0-0 with England at the World Cup. But their counterparts were subdued after England dominated possession but came up empty on several late scoring chances.

video:video.video:title: South African inmates embrace art in prison to curb repeat offending
video:video.video:description: When most people think of prison, they picture steel bars, locked gates and lost freedom. But at a correctional facility in Johannesburg, South Africa’s largest city, visitors are greeted by something unexpected: an art gallery.

video:video.video:title: A huge Viking textile production site is unearthed in Denmark
video:video.video:description: Archaeologists say they have unearthed a “huge” Viking Age textile production site in Danish Jutland.

video:video.video:title: South African inmates embrace art in prison to curb repeat offending
video:video.video:description: When most people think of prison, they picture steel bars, locked gates and lost freedom. But at a correctional facility in Johannesburg, South Africa’s largest city, visitors are greeted by something unexpected: an art gallery

video:video.video:title: Mamdani slate sweeps Democratic primaries in New York, ousts 2 incumbents from Congress
video:video.video:description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s slate of fiery progressives swept establishment-backed Democrats in the state's congressional primaries on Tuesday, ousting two sitting congressmen.

video:video.video:title: Heat waves and heat domes: What to know about rising temperatures
video:video.video:description: Driven by human-caused climate change, heat waves around the world are “tending to last longer, be stronger, and often more intense,” experts say.

video:video.video:title: UN nuclear agency boss signals that inspectors will visit Iran's nuclear sites
video:video.video:description: The head of the U.N.'s nuclear agency signaled Wednesday that Iranian nuclear enrichment sites would be visited by his inspectors, a key component in the interim deal between the United States and Iran to reach an end to the war.

video:video.video:title: A huge Viking textile production site is unearthed in Denmark
video:video.video:description: Archaeologists say they have unearthed a Viking Age textile production site in Danish Jutland. The sprawling 100,000-square-meter site features an area for processing flax, plus more than 80 pit houses - semi-submerged huts that were used as workshops and dwellings during the Viking Age

video:video.video:title: North Korea's Kim claims progress on nuclear-armed navy as new warship is placed into service
video:video.video:description: North Korea has commissioned a 5,000-ton destroyer that leader Kim Jong Un touts as a symbol of the country’s growing naval and nuclear capabilities, state media reported Wednesday, as Pyongyang seeks to expand its ability to project military power at sea.

video:video.video:title: Pharrell presents Louis Vuitton Spring 2027 collection
video:video.video:description: Celebrities including BamBam and J-Hope attended the Louis Vuitton Men's Spring/Summer 2027 fashion show presented by creative director Pharrell Williams. (June 24)

video:video.video:title: Tips for staying safe in a rip current have changed
video:video.video:description: Rip currents aren't always easy to detect. Sometimes they're connected to stormy weather but other times they can occur during sunny days even in calm-looking waters.

video:video.video:title: Meet the team tailoring spacesuits for lunar astronauts
video:video.video:description: If all goes as planned, NASA is planning to send astronauts back to the moon in 2028. Artemis IV will be the first time humans have returned to the lunar surface since Apollo 17 in 1972.

video:video.video:title: Horror 'Human Vapor' turns humans into gas
video:video.video:description: Director Shinzo Katayama of upcoming Japanese Netflix horror series "Human Vapor" explains how he utilized FX to turn his human characters into gas. (June 24)

video:video.video:title: Mamdani-backed candidates sweep Democratic primaries in New York
video:video.video:description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani waded into Democratic U.S. House primaries to boost three progressives over establishment-backed candidates.

video:video.video:title: European heatwave triggers health warnings from London to Milan as temperatures rise even higher
video:video.video:description: Large parts of western Europe were baking Wednesday as a "heat dome" brought extreme conditions that many forecasters warned could present a risk to life.

video:video.video:title: Lebanese on the edge of Israel’s occupation live with fear and rising tensions
video:video.video:description: Jdeidat Marjayoun is one of a string of towns and villages visited by The Associated Press on the blurry edge of the Israeli-occupied zone of southern Lebanon.

video:video.video:title: Displaced Nabatiyeh resident returns to find little of her home remains
video:video.video:description: Hanan Qubaisi climbs carefully over piles of concrete, twisted metal and broken furniture, searching through the rubble that was once her family home in the southern Lebanese city of Nabatiyeh.

video:video.video:title: Camp Mystic files for bankruptcy after catastrophic floods killed 28 people
video:video.video:description: Camp Mystic filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy reorganization on Wednesday, nearly a year after catastrophic floods killed 25 girls and two teenage counselors at the all-girls Christian camp in Texas.

video:video.video:title: Serbia's raspberry growers face climate change and low prices
video:video.video:description: Serbia is one of the world's biggest raspberry producers. In one town that relies on the fruit, climate change and unstable purchase prices are making it harder to do business.

video:video.video:title: Europe swelters in hot, humid weather and UK gets red alert for extreme heat
video:video.video:description: Millions of people across Europe were exposed to extreme and exceptional high temperatures on Tuesday, with 40 fatalities from drowning recorded in France the past week as residents seek relief from the searing heat.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories June 24
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Wednesday June 24th: Three Mamdani-backed progressive candidates win House primaries in New York; UN nuclear agency boss says inspectors will visit Iran's nuclear sites; Senate passes war powers resolution; Scottish World Cup fans descend on Miami.

video:video.video:title: Robots used by Amazon keep packages moving quickly
video:video.video:description: Look inside Amazon's robotics lab in Massachusetts, where a fleet of robots keeps packages moving swiftly. (AP video: Sydney Roth)

video:video.video:title: Smart tech powered by AI is presented by Samsung at VivaTech
video:video.video:description: From smart rings that track sleep to refrigerators that recognize food and suggest recipes, Samsung is using AI to connect more of its products together.

video:video.video:title: Thick black smoke rises from large fire in Houston
video:video.video:description: A massive fire was reported in southeast Houston on Monday afternoon, sending black smoke billowing for miles across the city, according to aerial footage from Houston TV station KTRK.

video:video.video:title: Trump insists there will be nuclear inspections in Iran, but 'there's no rush'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Tuesday that Iran agreed to U.N. inspections of its nuclear sites and warned he would halt talks if Tehran refused.

video:video.video:title: Why is the World Cup so long?
video:video.video:description: We're a few weeks into the FIFA World Cup and you're probably asking, "Why is the World Cup so long?" Here's the answer.

video:video.video:title: Incumbent who led Trump impeachment faces Mamdani-backed challenger in NY Democratic primary
video:video.video:description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani stopped Tuesday outside a polling site on the Lower East Side for an appearance with congressional candidate Brad Lander, a fellow Democrat.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 23
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Tuesday, June 23rd: President Trump says Iran agreed to nuclear inspections; Pakistan expresses happiness for U.S.

video:video.video:title: 18-year-old suspect arrested in shooting at California library that killed 2
video:video.video:description: A commuter recorded the police response after a shooting at a library in Northern California left two people dead and an 18-year-old suspect under arrest.

video:video.video:title: Temperatures in the UK soar as heatwave grips parts of Europe
video:video.video:description: Seeking immediate relief from blistering temperatures, scores of Britons flocked to beaches and riverbanks on Tuesday to escape Europe's heatwave.

video:video.video:title: Democrats pledge to continue pressing Trump and GOP on Iran war and affordability
video:video.video:description: House Democratic Leader Hakeem Jeffries has strongly criticized Republicans and the Trump administration for a “reckless and costly” military engagement in the Middle East.

video:video.video:title: Senate approves a war powers resolution in a rebuke to Trump over Iran
video:video.video:description: The Senate for the first time has approved a war powers resolution to block U.S. military action against Iran.

video:video.video:title: Accra erupts with excitement as Ghana holds England scoreless, despite flurry-filled final minutes
video:video.video:description: Soccer fans gathered in the Ghanaian capital, Accra on Tuesday to watch their national team draw 0-0 with England in the World Cup.

video:video.video:title: New San Francisco museum explores Chinese LGBTQ+ art and history
video:video.video:description: America's oldest Chinatown is now home to a first-of-its-kind museum focused on LGBTQ+ people of Chinese descent.

video:video.video:title: Sharp drops in Big Tech companies pull the Nasdaq lower
video:video.video:description: A sell-off in big technology companies spread from Asia back to the U.S., pulling the Nasdaq composite down 3% in early trading.

video:video.video:title: Larry the cat, British unofficial first feline, has served under 6 prime ministers
video:video.video:description: In turbulent political times, stability comes with four legs, whiskers and a fondness for napping. Larry the cat, the British government’s official rodent-catcher and unofficial first feline, is a reassuring presence who has served under six prime ministers.

video:video.video:title: How Brexit broke British politics
video:video.video:description: Brexit fractured the European Union, and broke British politics. The U.K. is about to get its seventh prime minister since June 23, 2016, a decade ago Tuesday, when the country voted 52%-48% to leave the EU after more than four decades of membership.

video:video.video:title: Guinness crowns Canberra town crier as the world's loudest person
video:video.video:description: Guinness World Records last week acknowledged 58-year-old Joseph McGrail-Bateup from Canberra, Australia resident as the world’s loudest person.

video:video.video:title: Guinness crowns Canberra town crier as the world's loudest person at 122.4 decibels
video:video.video:description: Joseph McGrail-Bateup, an Australian professional air conditioner cleaner and honorary town crier, has been recognized as the world’s loudest person.

video:video.video:title: 40 mayors worldwide endorse a pact to shape data center development
video:video.video:description: Forty mayors from around the world have signed onto a new pact to try to shape how urban data centers are built and operated. (AP Video: Aya Diab)

video:video.video:title: Mayor Mamdani thrilled as his support helps candidates sweep Democratic primaries in New York
video:video.video:description: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani is thrilled following the results of Tuesday’s New York congressional primary elections.

video:video.video:title: A member of the cultlike Zizians group is charged in the killings of her parents in Pennsylvania
video:video.video:description: A Pennsylvania prosecutor says a member of the cultlike group known as Zizians has been charged in the killing of her parents in 2022.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories June 24
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Wednesday, June 24th: Trump halts signing the housing bill; Federal judge blocks Trump’s proof of citizenship requirement for voting.; Chief of staff to Eric Adams, charged with bribery; Western Europe is experiencing extreme heat.

video:video.video:title: NBA Draft prospects describe their outfits in three words
video:video.video:description: 2026 NBA Draft prospects talk fashion with AP!

video:video.video:title: Strong earthquake jolts Northern California, no injuries reported
video:video.video:description: A rural area of Northern California experienced its strongest earthquake since 1940 on Wednesday morning, but it caused only mild shaking with no immediate reports of damage or injuries.

video:video.video:title: Which NBA players do 2026 Draft Prospects want to play against?
video:video.video:description: Hear who the 2026 NBA Draft prospects want to go up against!

video:video.video:title: Trump hails 'great' GOP Senate meeting after calling off bipartisan housing bill signing
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said he had a "really great meeting" with Senate Republicans Wednesday after a tense GOP luncheon and his abrupt cancellation of a bipartisan housing bill signing — the latest sign of friction with congressional Republicans.

video:video.video:title: Colombia's vote may reshape the Amazon's future as political winds shift across Latin America
video:video.video:description: Abelardo de la Espriella, set to be Colombia’s president, has intensified debate over the future of the Amazon.

video:video.video:title: Progressive candidate concedes Colombian presidential election to Trump-backed outsider Abelardo de la Espriella
video:video.video:description: Progressive candidate Iván Cepeda on Wednesday conceded Colombia’s presidential election to Abelardo de la Espriella, a conservative outsider who was endorsed by U.S.

video:video.video:title: Fleet of international tall ships arrive in Baltimore for America 250 celebrations
video:video.video:description: Sail250 continues its East Coast tour, offering free tours of tall ships and airshows to commemorate the U.S. semiquincentennial. (AP video by Mingson Lau)

video:video.video:title: Super fan honors African leader's legacy at World Cup
video:video.video:description: Congo’s famous living statue finally made his World Cup debut on Tuesday. Michel Nkuka Mboladinga, who gained fame during the Africa Cup of Nations for posing as a statue of Congo’s assassinated independence leader Patrice Lumumba for the entirety of games, attended Congo’s 1-0 loss to Colombia

video:video.video:title: Rutte praises Trump's actions on Iran, insists NATO allies are with him
video:video.video:description: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte praised President Donald Trump’s actions in the war against Iran during an Oval Office meeting Wednesday, while pushing back on Trump’s criticism of the alliance and insisting NATO allies backed him in the conflict.

video:video.video:title: Powerful earthquake hits Venezuela, swaying buildings in the capital
video:video.video:description: A powerful 7.1-magnitude earthquake shook Venezuela on Wednesday evening, collapsing buildings in the capital of Caracas. (AP/ Juan Arraez)

video:video.video:title: Trump on New York Democratic primary wins
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump slammed some of the New York Democratic primary victors Wednesday insisting socialism has "never, ever worked."

video:video.video:title: Powerful 6.9-magnitude earthquake strikes off northern Japan, no tsunami warnings
video:video.video:description: A powerful earthquake struck off Japan’s northern coast on Thursday, the meteorological agency said.

video:video.video:title: How one Indian textile worker copes with stifling factory work and cramped living quarters
video:video.video:description: One of the groups worst affected by India’s climate-driven extreme summer heat is migrant workers whose jobs require them to work in hot factory floors.

video:video.video:title: Mud caked devotees wrapped in dried banana leaves honor St. John the Baptist
video:video.video:description: Devotees covered in mud and banana leaves took part in the annual Taong Putik festival in the Philippine village of Bibiclat, honoring St.

video:video.video:title: Tom Holland and Zendaya greet 'Spider-Man' fans in Paris
video:video.video:description: Tom Holland and Zendaya meet excited fans in Paris as part of their “Spider-Man: Brand New Day" world tour.

video:video.video:title: Trump praises US military operation in Iran and capture of ex-Venezuelan president Maduro
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump praised the U.S. military's performance in Iran and the operation that led to the capture and arrest of former Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro in January.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he doesn’t ‘know anything’ about Andy Burnham, who could be the UK’s next prime minister
video:video.video:description: "I hear he's extremely liberal," Trump said about Burnham who has been the mayor of Greater Manchester, which has a population in excess of 3 million.

video:video.video:title: Morocco and Haiti fans celebrate together the outcome of their World Cup game
video:video.video:description: Fans reacted on Wednesday after Morocco rallied for a 4-2 victory over Haiti and erased the Caribbean nation’s hopes of a first ever point at the World Cup.

video:video.video:title: A push for pets in hospice wards in Poland
video:video.video:description: Patients in hospitals could soon have the right to visits from their own pets under a proposed new law in Poland.

video:video.video:title: Seth Rogen: 'Make your own stuff'
video:video.video:description: Seth Rogen says recent hits "Obsession" and "Backrooms" are part of a tradition of young creatives building their own doors into Hollywood.

video:video.video:title: Johnny Knoxville on 'trying to make 'Tom & Jerry' real'
video:video.video:description: Johnny Knoxville may not put himself in harm's way in "Jackass: Best and Last," but says he's got a new perspective looking back on his prank- and stunt-filled franchise.

video:video.video:title: Back-to-back powerful earthquakes slam Venezuela, collapsing buildings in the capital of Caracas
video:video.video:description: Back-to-back powerful earthquakes slammed Venezuela on Wednesday evening, collapsing buildings in the capital of Caracas and leaving residents shaken.

video:video.video:title: Hindu artisans in Karachi prepare Tazias as part of religious festival of Ashura
video:video.video:description: In a bustling core colonial area of Pakistan’s port city of Karachi, a minority Hindu family is busy preparing Tazia, a symbolic tomb of Imam Hussain, a grandson of Prophet Muhammad to commemorate the historic martyrdom alongside the country's Muslim majority.

video:video.video:title: House lawmakers welcome Taiwanese delegation to Capitol Hill
video:video.video:description: A bipartisan group of U.S. House lawmakers welcomed a delegation from Taiwan to Capitol Hill Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Fans travel to see Switzerland's 2-1 victory over Canada in the World Cup
video:video.video:description: Red jerseys and flags filled the streets around BC Place after the match, as Swiss and Canadian fans brought a lively postgame atmosphere to downtown Vancouver.

video:video.video:title: Venezuela's acting president declares state of emergency following earthquakes
video:video.video:description: Powerful back-to-back earthquakes struck Venezuela on Wednesday evening, collapsing buildings and sending panicked residents into the streets as communities across the South American country sustained damage.

video:video.video:title: Acting Venezuela President Rodríguez says 32 dead and 700 injured after earthquakes with more casualties expected
video:video.video:description: Powerful back-to-back earthquakes struck Venezuela on Wednesday evening, collapsing buildings and sending panicked residents into the streets as communities across the South American country sustained damage.

video:video.video:title: Germany and Ecuador World Cup fans fill New York’s Times Square with flags, drums, music and noise
video:video.video:description: Fans of the Germany and Ecuador World Cup teams filled New York City’s Times Square on Wednesday, ahead of Thursday’s World Cup match.

video:video.video:title: Fans erupt in celebrations as Mexico beats Czech Republic 3-0 to complete perfect group stage
video:video.video:description: Thousands of Mexican fans celebrated in the rain after their team's victory over the Czech Republic 3-0 on Wednesday to reach the World Cup round of 32.
